steps.title: Identify the dual benefits
steps.content: The passage emphasizes both community benefit (“can promote learning”) and research benefit (“very effective” at increasing data). These are connected by “also,” signaling they work together as complementary advantages.

steps.title: Match both benefits
steps.content:
The correct answer directly captures both: the approach “can increase learning outside the classroom and generate large amounts of data, benefiting both communities and researchers.” The other choices contradict one benefit each (A denies learning; D suggests data collection is unreliable), while B reduces the main idea to a single example.

That is the correct answer.
